title: Occupational Therapy Aides
description: Under close supervision of an occupational therapist or occupational therapy assistant, perform only delegated, selected, or routine tasks in specific situations. These duties include preparing patient and treatment room.

tasks.text: Encourage patients and attend to their physical needs to facilitate the attainment of therapeutic goals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Report to supervisors or therapists, verbally or in writing, on patients' progress, attitudes, attendance, and accomplishments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Observe patients' attendance, progress, attitudes, and accomplishments and record and maintain information in client records.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare and maintain work area, materials, and equipment and maintain inventory of treatment and educational supplies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Transport patients to and from the occupational therapy work area.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Instruct patients and families in work, social, and living skills, the care and use of adaptive equipment, and other skills to facilitate home and work adjustment to disability.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assist occupational therapists in planning, implementing, and administering therapy programs to restore, reinforce, and enhance performance, using selected activities and special equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Demonstrate therapy techniques, such as manual and creative arts and games.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Manage intradepartmental infection control and equipment security.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform clerical, administrative, and secretarial duties, such as answering phones, restocking and ordering supplies, filling out paperwork, and scheduling appointments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise patients in choosing and completing work assignments or arts and crafts projects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Adjust and repair assistive devices and make adaptive changes to other equipment and to environments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate the living skills and capacities of clients with physical, developmental, or mental health disabilities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Accompany patients on outings, providing transportation when necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assist educational specialists or clinical psychologists in administering situational or diagnostic tests to measure client's abilities or progress.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
